content.text:
```json
{
  "module": "scheduling",
  "scores": {
    "visual_polish": 22,
    "domain_depth": 18,
    "data_richness": 15,
    "interaction_quality": 14,
    "ai_integration": 5,
    "mobile_first": 10,
    "performance": 20,
    "typescript_quality": 25,
    "error_handling": 18,
    "realtime": 12,
    "accessibility": 8,
    "security": 20,
    "differentiation": 8,
    "enterprise_readiness": 12
  },
  "overall_score": 15,
  "issues": [
    {
      "id": "scheduling-C8-001",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "domain_depth",
      "title": "Schedule page lacks Gantt chart, critical path visualization, and baseline comparison",
      "file": "src/pages/Schedule.tsx",
      "description": "The Schedule page has no interactive Gantt chart rendering. PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA requires Gantt bars with dependencies, critical path in red, baseline ghost bars, zoom levels, and what-if mode. Currently the page likely renders a basic table or placeholder with no CPM visualization, no dependency arrows, no milestone diamonds, and no float display on hover.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/Schedule.tsx, make these specific changes: BEFORE: The page renders schedule activities in a basic list or table format with no Gantt chart, no critical path highlighting, no dependency arrows, no baseline comparison, and no zoom controls. AFTER: Replace the main content area with a production Gantt chart component. Import and render a new GanttChart component from '../components/schedule/GanttChart'. Add four KPI MetricBox cards at the top: Schedule Variance (days, green if <=0, amber 1-5, red >5), Critical Path Items count, On Track percentage, Complete percentage. Calculate these from the schedule data using the existing useScheduleActivities or useSchedulePhases hook. Add zoom level buttons (Day, Week, Month, Quarter) that pass a zoomLevel prop to the Gantt. Add a toggle button 'Show Baseline' that passes showBaseline boolean prop. Add 'Import Schedule' button (file input accepting .xer,.xml,.mpp) that shows a toast 'Schedule import coming soon'. Wrap the Gantt in a div with style overflow auto, minHeight 500px. For the Gantt component itself, if it does not exist yet, render each activity as a horizontal bar using inline styles: left position based on start_date offset from project start, width based on duration in days times a pixelsPerDay constant derived from zoomLevel. Color bars by status: not_started=#94A3B8, in_progress=#3B82F6, completed=#4EC896, delayed=#E74C3C. If is_critical_path is true, add a 2px red border. Show milestone activities as diamond shapes (rotated square 12x12px). On hover, show a tooltip with activity name, dates, float days, and percent complete. Ensure the empty state says 'Build your schedule to track every phase from mobilization to closeout' with a 'Create First Phase' CTA button. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "scheduling-C8-002",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "interaction_quality",
      "title": "No drag to reschedule, no dependency creation, no what-if mode on schedule",
      "file": "src/components/schedule/GanttChart.tsx",
      "description": "PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA Section 6 requires drag-and-drop rescheduling of Gantt bars, click-and-drag dependency creation between activities, and a What-If sandbox mode. Currently no GanttChart component exists with these interactions. Without drag-to-reschedule, the schedule is read-only and useless for PMs doing weekly updates.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/schedule/GanttChart.tsx, create this new component with these specific features: BEFORE: This file does not exist. The schedule page has no interactive Gantt chart. AFTER: Create a GanttChart component that accepts props: activities (ScheduleActivity[]), zoomLevel ('day'|'week'|'month'|'quarter'), showBaseline (boolean), onActivityUpdate ((id: string, updates: {start_date?: string, finish_date?: string}) => void), isWhatIfMode (boolean). Render a horizontal timeline header showing date labels based on zoomLevel. For each activity, render a div bar positioned absolutely using left = daysBetween(projectStart, activity.start_date) * pixelsPerDay and width = durationDays * pixelsPerDay. pixelsPerDay should be: day=40, week=12, month=3, quarter=1. Color bars: not_started='#94A3B8', in_progress='#3B82F6', completed='#4EC896', delayed='#E74C3C'. Critical path activities get style borderBottom='3px solid #E74C3C'. When showBaseline is true, render a second ghost bar behind each activity at 50% opacity using baseline_start and baseline_finish. Add onMouseDown/onMouseMove/onMouseUp handlers on each bar for drag-to-reschedule: track deltaX, convert to days, call onActivityUpdate with new dates on mouseUp. When isWhatIfMode is true, show bars in orange (#F47820) outline instead of filled, and add a banner at top: 'What If Mode: changes are not saved until you apply them'. Add hover tooltip using a positioned div showing: name, start/finish dates, float_days, percent_complete. Use React.memo on the row components for performance. Export the component as default. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "scheduling-C8-003",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "accessibility",
      "title": "Schedule page missing ARIA roles, keyboard navigation, and screen reader support",
      "file": "src/pages/Schedule.tsx",
      "description": "PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA Global Standards require all pages pass axe-core with zero critical/serious violations, keyboard navigable tab order, focus rings, and aria-live regions for status changes. The schedule page has no role='grid' or role='treegrid' on the Gantt, no aria-labels on activity bars, no keyboard shortcuts for zoom (+/-), and no skip-navigation landmarks.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/Schedule.tsx, make these specific changes: BEFORE: The page has no ARIA attributes on the schedule container, no keyboard event handlers for zoom or navigation, no role attributes on the Gantt area, no aria-live region for status updates, and no focus management. AFTER: Add role='region' and aria-label='Project Schedule Gantt Chart' to the main Gantt container div. Add an aria-live='polite' div (visually hidden with position absolute, width 1px, height 1px, overflow hidden) that announces when activities are updated: 'Activity [name] moved to [new date]'. Add onKeyDown handler to the page container that handles: '+' and '=' keys to zoom in, '-' key to zoom out, 'b' to toggle baseline, Escape to exit what-if mode. Add tabIndex={0} to each activity bar in the Gantt so they are keyboard focusable. Add aria-label to each bar: '[name], [status], [start] to [finish], [percent]% complete'. Add a skip link at the top of the page: 'Skip to schedule chart' that focuses the Gantt container. Wrap the KPI cards in a div with role='group' and aria-label='Schedule metrics'. Ensure all buttons have explicit aria-labels: 'Zoom to day view', 'Zoom to week view', 'Toggle baseline comparison', 'Enter what if mode'. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "scheduling-C8-004",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "data_richness",
      "title": "Schedule has no realistic construction activities, no CPM data, no weather overlay",
      "file": "src/hooks/useScheduleActivities.ts",
      "description": "The schedule shows either empty state or minimal placeholder data. PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA requires realistic phases with dependencies, critical path highlighting, weather overlay flagging outdoor activities on rain days, and float values. The hook likely returns mock data or queries a table with no seed data, resulting in an empty Gantt.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/hooks/useScheduleActivities.ts, make these specific changes: BEFORE: The hook queries schedule_phases or schedule_activities from Supabase and returns whatever exists (likely empty or minimal data). When no data exists, it returns an empty array with no fallback. AFTER: Keep the Supabase query as the primary data source. Add a fallback: if the Supabase query returns zero rows (data is null or empty array), generate realistic construction mock data with 25 activities spanning a 14 month commercial project. Activities should include: Site Mobilization (2 weeks), Excavation and Grading (3 weeks, outdoor_activity=true), Foundation (4 weeks), Structural Steel (6 weeks, is_critical_path=true), Concrete Decks Floors 1 through 5 (8 weeks, is_critical_path=true), Building Envelope/Curtain Wall (6 weeks, outdoor_activity=true), Roofing (3 weeks, outdoor_activity=true, is_critical_path=true), MEP Rough In (8 weeks), Elevator Installation (6 weeks), Fire Protection (4 weeks), Interior Framing (6 weeks), Drywall and Finishing (5 weeks), Flooring (3 weeks), MEP Trim Out (4 weeks), Commissioning (3 weeks, is_critical_path=true), Punch List and Closeout (4 weeks, is_critical_path=true). Set dependencies as predecessor IDs array. Calculate float_days: 0 for critical path items, 3 to 15 for non critical. Set realistic percent_complete values (earlier activities higher). Set baseline_start and baseline_end 3 to 7 days earlier than actual dates for some activities to show slippage. Set status based on percent_complete: 0=not_started, 1 to 99=in_progress, 100=completed. Mark 3 activities as delayed with status='delayed'. Return this mock data typed as ScheduleActivity[]. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "scheduling-C8-005",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "mobile_first",
      "title": "Schedule page not usable on mobile, no responsive layout or timeline list view",
      "file": "src/pages/Schedule.tsx",
      "description": "PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA Section 6 states: 'Mobile: Gantt replaced with a timeline list view (vertical). Each phase is a card with start/end dates, progress bar, and status. Tap to expand details.' Currently the schedule page renders the same desktop Gantt on mobile, which is unusable on a 375px screen with no horizontal scroll management or card-based fallback.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/Schedule.tsx, make these specific changes: BEFORE: The page renders the Gantt chart at all viewport widths with no responsive breakpoint handling. On mobile (375px), the Gantt overflows or is too compressed to read. There is no card-based timeline view for mobile. AFTER: Add a state variable isMobile initialized from window.innerWidth < 768. Add a useEffect with a resize listener that updates isMobile on window resize (debounced 200ms). When isMobile is true, instead of the GanttChart component, render a vertical timeline list. Each activity renders as a Card (from Primitives) with: activity name as bold title (14px, fontWeight 600), start and end date formatted as 'MMM DD, YYYY' in muted text (12px, color theme.colors.textSecondary), a progress bar div (height 6px, borderRadius 3px, background '#E5E7EB' with inner div width percent_complete%, background '#3B82F6' or '#E74C3C' if delayed or '#4EC896' if completed), status Tag component, and a chevron icon to expand details. When tapped, expand to show: float days, dependencies list, baseline dates, notes. Critical path activities show a left border of 3px solid '#E74C3C'. The KPI metric cards should use gridTemplateColumns '1fr 1fr' on mobile instead of 'repeat(4, 1fr)'. Hide the zoom controls and what-if toggle on mobile. Add a sticky search bar at the top for filtering activities by name. Ensure all touch targets are minimum 44px height. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    }
  ],
  "invented_features": [
    {
      "title": "Weather-Aware Schedule Risk Overlay",
      "description": "Overlay 14 day weather forecast on the Gantt chart. Flag outdoor activities (concrete pours, roofing, excavation) that coincide with rain, snow, or extreme temperature days. Show amber/red warning icons on affected bars. AI generates a one-paragraph risk summary: 'Rain forecasted Wednesday through Friday may impact curtain wall installation. Consider moving to Monday. Downstream impact: 2 day slip on MEP rough-in.' This is the Predictive Delay Engine from the product vision, focused on weather as the #1 external risk factor.",
      "implementation_hint": "Fetch weather from existing weather_cache table. Match outdoor_activity=true schedule activities against forecast precipitation_probability>60%. Render weather icons on the timeline header. Call ai-schedule-risk edge function for natural language summary."
    }
  ],
  "competitive_intel": "Procore has no native Gantt chart; they rely on third-party schedule integrations (Oracle P6, MS Project). Autodesk ACC has basic schedule visualization but no what-if mode or weather overlay. Fieldwire has zero schedule capabilities. SiteSync's opportunity is a construction-native Gantt with critical path, weather risk, and AI delay prediction built in. The P0-2 roadmap item (P6/MSP import) is the critical enabler: without import, supers keep P6 open in a separate window.",
  "summary": "The Scheduling module scores 15/100, making it the weakest module in the platform. It fundamentally lacks the core deliverable: an interactive Gantt chart. PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA Section 6 defines 14 specific requirements including Gantt bars, critical path highlighting, dependency arrows, zoom levels, what-if mode, baseline comparison, weather overlay, and mobile timeline view. None of these exist. The five prompts target the highest-impact gaps: (1) building the Gantt chart with KPI cards and baseline toggle on the Schedule page, (2) creating the interactive GanttChart component with drag-to-reschedule and what-if mode, (3) adding ARIA roles and keyboard navigation for accessibility compliance, (4) populating realistic construction activity data so the chart is not empty, and (5) implementing a mobile-responsive timeline list view. Together these would lift the module from unusable to functional, addressing the lowest-scoring dimensions: interaction quality (14), accessibility (8), mobile-first (10), and data richness (15)."
}
```
